tool_args.label: NYC DOT Better Buses Page

payload.description: NYC DOT Better Buses webpage describing the Better Buses Action Plan, Bus Priority Toolkit, and current bus priority projects across New York City.

tool_args.label: NYC Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

payload.agency_name: New York City Department of Transportation

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)
New York Police Department (NYPD)
Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

payload.entity_name: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT, in coordination with the MTA and the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

tool_args.label: Better Buses Action Plan

payload.description: The Better Buses Action Plan is focused on improving bus speeds citywide by 25% and reversing downward bus ridership trends. Accomplished through NYC DOT projects using the bus priority toolkit, increased camera and NYPD enforcement of bus lanes, and service management initiatives and bus network redesigns by MTA.

tool_args.label: Better Buses Action Plan goal: 25% bus speed improvement citywide

payload.raw_value_text: improving bus speeds citywide by 25%
payload.description: Citywide bus speed improvement target of the Better Buses Action Plan

evidence_refs.source_quote: focused on improving bus speeds citywide by 25%

tool_args.label: Goal: Improve 5 miles of existing bus lanes each year

payload.raw_value_text: Improve 5 miles of existing bus lanes each year
payload.description: Annual goal for improving existing bus lanes

evidence_refs.source_quote: Improve 5 miles of existing bus lanes each year

tool_args.label: Goal: Install 10-15 miles of new bus lanes each year

payload.raw_value_text: Install 10–15 miles of new bus lanes each year
payload.description: Annual goal for installing new bus lanes

evidence_refs.source_quote: Install 10–15 miles of new bus lanes each year

tool_args.label: Goal: Pilot up to 2 miles of physically separated bus lanes

payload.raw_value_text: Pilot up to 2 miles of physically separated bus lanes
payload.description: Pilot target for physically separated bus lanes

evidence_refs.source_quote: Pilot up to 2 miles of physically separated bus lanes

tool_args.label: Goal: Benefit 600,000 daily riders each year

payload.raw_value_text: 600,000 daily riders each year
payload.description: Annual goal for NYC DOT street design projects benefiting daily riders

evidence_refs.source_quote: Implement NYC DOT street design projects that benefit 600,000 daily riders each year

tool_args.label: Goal: Add 300 Transit Signal Priority intersections per year

payload.raw_value_text: Add 300 Transit Signal Priority (TSP) intersections per year
payload.description: Annual goal for adding Transit Signal Priority (TSP) intersections

evidence_refs.source_quote: Add 300 Transit Signal Priority (TSP) intersections per year

tool_args.label: Enforcement goals: Expand bus lane camera enforcement and NYPD tow teams

payload.claim_text: Enforcement goals include expanding bus lane camera enforcement and increased NYPD bus lane enforcement with seven dedicated tow truck teams
payload.description: Better Buses Action Plan enforcement goals

evidence_refs.source_quote: Expand bus lane camera enforcement Increased NYPD bus lane enforcement with seven dedicated tow truck teams

tool_args.label: MTA support goals

payload.claim_text: Working with MTA goals include: Support MTA Bus Network Redesign efforts with borough bus priority plans; Press for all-door boarding, restarting the SBS program, and other improvements to bus operations
payload.description: Better Buses Action Plan MTA support goals

evidence_refs.source_quote: Support MTA Bus Network Redesign efforts with borough bus priority plans Press for all-door boarding, restarting the SBS program, and other improvements to bus operations

tool_args.label: New Bus Lane

payload.description: Bus lanes separate buses from general traffic, improving speed and reliability. Typically located along the curb or 'offset' from the curb, allowing the curb lane to be utilized for other purposes. Enforcement is crucial.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Bus lanes separate buses from general traffic, improving speed and reliability. They are typically located along the curb or "offset" from the curb, allowing the curb lane to be utilized for other purposes.

tool_args.label: Upgraded Bus Lane

payload.description: Improving bus lanes to make them more effective, including extending hours, painting the lanes red, changing curbside lanes to offset lanes, or other measures.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT sometimes considers improving bus lanes to make them more effective. This may include extending hours, painting the lanes red, changing curbside lanes to offset lanes, or other measures.

tool_args.label: Potential Protected Bus Lane

payload.description: Surrounding a bus lane with a physical barrier makes it more difficult for other vehicles to illegally use the lane. Must be accompanied by measures that allow access to the curb for emergency vehicles.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Surrounding a bus lane with a physical barrier makes it more difficult for other vehicles to illegally use the lane.

tool_args.label: Bus Boarders

payload.description: Bus bulbs are permanent sidewalk extensions at bus stops that provide more space for waiting passengers and allow buses to save time by pulling up to the curb without leaving the travel lane. Durable recycled plastic 'bus boarders' serve the same purpose without capital construction.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Bus bulbs are permanent sidewalk extensions at bus stops that provide more space for waiting passengers and allow buses to save time by pulling up to the curb without leaving the travel lane.

tool_args.label: Bus Queue Jump Signal

payload.description: Dedicated bus signal phases that provide buses with a dedicated signal phase allowing them to bypass congested general traffic and get a head start.

evidence_refs.source_quote: These bus queue jump signals provide buses with a dedicated signal phase that allows them to bypass congested general traffic and get a head start.

tool_args.label: Traffic Flow Improvement

payload.description: Improving the general flow of traffic with signal timing adjustments or improved lane markings can also benefit buses.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Improving the general flow of traffic with signal timing adjustments or improved lane markings can also benefit buses.

tool_args.label: Curb Management

payload.description: Adequate and appropriate truck loading, passenger drop-off, and parking regulations can benefit bus operations by reducing double parking and the illegal use of bus lanes. Effective enforcement is also critical.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Adequate and appropriate truck loading, passenger drop-off, and parking regulations can benefit bus operations by reducing double parking and the illegal use of bus lanes.

tool_args.label: Pedestrian Safety

payload.description: Under Vision Zero, the City uses every tool to improve street safety. Bus priority projects such as bus bulbs shorten pedestrian crossings and improve bus operations, and new crosswalks and median refuge islands can improve safety for all pedestrians.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Bus priority projects such as bus bulbs shorten pedestrian crossings and improve bus operations, and new crosswalks and median refuge islands can improve safety for all pedestrians, including bus riders accessing stops.

tool_args.label: Bus Stop Accessibility

payload.description: Bus stops need to be accessible to everyone. Nearby sidewalks and pedestrian ramps should be present and in good condition. NYC DOT reviews and updates sidewalks and pedestrian ramps during bus priority projects.

evidence_refs.source_quote: bus stops need to be accessible to everyone. This means that nearby sidewalks and pedestrian ramps should be present and in good condition.

tool_args.label: Turn Restrictions

payload.description: Limiting certain turns increases safety for all users. In certain projects doing so also allows buses to avoid conflicts and move faster. Turn restrictions also benefit traffic flow.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Limiting certain turns is another way that the City can increase safety for all users. In certain projects doing so also allows buses to avoid conflicts and move faster.

tool_args.label: Busway

payload.description: Busways prioritize travel for buses and often trucks, with other motorized vehicles limited to local access. This treatment significantly reduces traffic volumes and congestion, improving bus speeds and reliability, while improving safety for all roadway users.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Busways prioritize travel for buses and often trucks, with other motorized vehicles limited to local access.

tool_args.label: Broadway, 69th Street to Roosevelt Avenue Bus Lane, Queens

payload.description: Proposing an eastbound center-running bus lane on Broadway in Queens between 69th Street and Roosevelt Avenue. Will speed up bus service for approximately 9,000 daily riders on the Q70 SBS and provide improved transportation from LaGuardia Airport to the Jackson Heights – Roosevelt Avenue subway station and bus terminal.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT, in coordination with the MTA and the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey, is proposing an eastbound center-running bus lane on Broadway in Queens between 69th Street and Roosevelt Avenue.

tool_args.label: Lexington Avenue, 60th Street to 52nd Street Offset Bus Lane, Manhattan

payload.project_name: Lexington Avenue, 60th Street to 52nd Street Offset Bus Lane
payload.description: NYC DOT proposes converting the existing curbside bus lane into an offset bus lane on this eight-block stretch. Moving the bus lane away from the curb will reduce conflict between buses and other vehicles trying to access the curb, and improve bus operations.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT proposes converting the existing curbside bus lane into an offset bus lane on this eight-block stretch.

tool_args.label: Marcy Avenue, Borinquen Place to Broadway Bus Priority Lane, Brooklyn

payload.project_name: Marcy Avenue, Borinquen Place to Broadway Bus Priority Lane
payload.description: Proposing to install a bus priority lane along the three blocks of Marcy Avenue between Borinquen Place and Broadway to alleviate delays for B24, B60, and Q54 bus routes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT is proposing to install a bus priority lane along the three blocks of Marcy Avenue between Borinquen Place and Broadway.

tool_args.label: Madison Avenue Bus Improvement Project, Manhattan

payload.description: Bus priority improvements on Madison Avenue from East 23rd Street to East 42nd Street. Includes extending existing double bus lanes south, left turn bays at select cross streets, and curb regulation changes. Aims to improve speeds and reliability for 92,000 daily bus riders on 34 local and express bus routes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT, in collaboration with MTA, is proposing bus priority improvements on Madison Avenue from East 23rd Street to East 42nd Street.

tool_args.label: Eastern Parkway Accessibility Improvements, Brooklyn

payload.project_name: Eastern Parkway from Schenectady Avenue to Ralph Avenue Accessibility Improvements
payload.description: Proposing accessibility improvements along Eastern Parkway from Schenectady Avenue to Ralph Avenue. Aims to create ADA-accessible ramps and bus stops, as well as improve B14 reliability.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT, in collaboration with MTA, is proposing accessibility improvements along Eastern Parkway, from Schenectady Avenue to Ralph Avenue.

tool_args.label: 79th Street SBS Capital Project, Manhattan

payload.description: Transit and pedestrian safety improvements along 79th Street from Riverside Drive to the FDR Drive. Follows up on NYC DOT's 2017 Street Improvement Project for M79 Select Bus Service. Includes bus bulbs, upgraded bus stops, new bus pads, updated ADA elements, curb extensions, improved median pedestrian refuges, refurbished sidewalks, stormwater infiltration basins, and new landscaping.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT is planning to install transit and pedestrian safety improvements along 79th Street from Riverside Drive to the FDR Drive as part of the 79th Street SBS Capital Project.

tool_args.label: Victory Boulevard Bus Service and Safety Improvements, Staten Island

payload.project_name: Victory Boulevard from Bay Street to Wild Avenue Bus Service Improvements
payload.description: NYC DOT and MTA seek to improve bus service and safety for all mode users on Victory Boulevard on Staten Island. Various treatments will be studied to find the best balance of all modes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT and MTA seek to improve bus service and safety for all mode users on Victory Boulevard on Staten Island.

tool_args.label: Broadway, 157th Street to 220th Street Study, Manhattan

payload.description: Exploring a potential project on Broadway between 157th and 220th St in cooperation with MTA. Identified by the NYC Streets Plan. Study will identify ways to improve bus speeds and reliability, as well as general safety and traffic flow improvements.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT is exploring a potential project on Broadway between 157th and 220th St in cooperation with MTA.

tool_args.label: 116th Street Bus Priority and Pedestrian Safety Study, Manhattan

payload.project_name: 116th Street, Morningside Avenue to Pleasant Avenue Study
payload.description: Studying bus priority and pedestrian safety improvements on 116th Street, Manhattan Avenue, Morningside Avenue and Pleasant Avenue. Study area serves 10 bus routes carrying 65,000+ daily riders, with connections to the 2, 3, 6, B, C trains and seven perpendicular bus routes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT is studying bus priority and pedestrian safety improvements on 116th Street, Manhattan Avenue, Morningside Avenue and Pleasant Avenue in Manhattan.

tool_args.label: Linden Boulevard Bus Priority and Traffic Safety Improvements, Brooklyn

payload.project_name: Linden Boulevard, Fountain Avenue to Conduit Avenue
payload.description: Proposing bus priority and traffic safety improvements on Linden Boulevard in Brooklyn and Queens from Fountain Avenue to Conduit Avenue. Seeks to improve bus service for 38,000 bus passengers using four bus routes on this Vision Zero corridor.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT is proposing bus priority and traffic safety improvements on Linden Boulevard in Brooklyn and Queens from Fountain Avenue to Conduit Avenue.

tool_args.label: 34th Street Enhanced Bus Priority, Manhattan

payload.description: Exploring bus and pedestrian safety enhancements on 34th Street, located within the Congestion Pricing tolling zone. Identified as an important corridor for facilitating safe and easy travel within the Central Business District. Aims to improve bus speeds and reliability, and safety.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT, in collaboration with MTA, is exploring bus and pedestrian safety enhancements on 34th Street.

tool_args.label: Dekalb & Lafayette Avenues B38 Bus Priority, Brooklyn

payload.description: NYC DOT and MTA seek to improve B38 bus service and pedestrian and cyclist safety on Dekalb and Lafayette Avenues. Various treatments will be studied to find the best balance of all modes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT and MTA seek to improve B38 bus service and pedestrian and cyclist safety on Dekalb and Lafayette Avenues.

tool_args.label: Bay Parkway and Cropsey Avenue Bus Priority and Safety Improvements, Brooklyn

payload.project_name: Bay Parkway and Cropsey Avenue Bus Priority and Safety Improvements
payload.description: Proposing bus priority and safety improvements on Bay Parkway from Avenue J to Shore Parkway as well as Cropsey Avenue from Bay Parkway to 26th Avenue. Aims to improve bus service and reliability for 35,000 daily bus riders.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT, in collaboration with the MTA, is proposing bus priority and safety improvements on Bay Parkway from Avenue J to Shore Parkway as well as Cropsey Avenue from Bay Parkway to 26th Avenue.

tool_args.label: Grand Street/Grand Avenue Bus Priority, Queens/Brooklyn

payload.project_name: Grand Street/Grand Avenue, Metropolitan Avenue to Queens Boulevard
payload.description: Proposing safety and bus priority improvements on Grand Street/Grand Avenue between Metropolitan Avenue and Queens Boulevard. Grand Street/Grand Avenue is a Vision Zero priority corridor that serves over 51,000 daily bus riders on the Q54, Q58, and Q59 bus routes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT, in collaboration with MTA, is proposing safety and bus priority improvements on Grand Street/Grand Avenue between Metropolitan Avenue and Queens Boulevard.

tool_args.label: Woodhaven Boulevard Transit and Pedestrian Safety Improvements, Queens

payload.project_name: Woodhaven Boulevard, Queens Boulevard to 107th Avenue
payload.description: Proposing transit and pedestrian safety improvements along Woodhaven Boulevard from Queens Boulevard to 107th Avenue. Improvements would be installed as part of three separate capital projects, beginning with Woodhaven Boulevard Phase B (Union Turnpike to 107th Avenue), estimated to start construction in 2026. Includes curb extensions, median widenings, raised crosswalks, and new landscaping.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT is proposing transit and pedestrian safety improvements along Woodhaven Boulevard in Queens, from Queens Boulevard to 107th Avenue.

tool_args.label: Tremont Avenue Bus Priority Project, the Bronx

payload.description: Proposing bus priority improvements on Tremont Avenue in the Bronx to improve travel speed and reliability for nearly 34,000 daily bus riders on the Bronx's 5th busiest bus route.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT, in cooperation with MTA, is proposing bus priority improvements on Tremont Avenue in the Bronx to improve travel speed and reliability for nearly 34,000 daily bus riders on the Bronx's 5th busiest bus route.

tool_args.label: Flatbush Avenue Bus Priority, Brooklyn

payload.description: Investigating bus priority and pedestrian safety improvements on Flatbush Avenue between Tillary Street and Avenue V. Flatbush Avenue is a future bus priority corridor from the NYC/MTA Transit Improvement Summit, a priority corridor in the NYC Streets Plan, and a Vision Zero priority corridor.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT, in cooperation with MTA, is investigating bus priority and pedestrian safety improvements on Flatbush Avenue between Tillary Street and Avenue V.

tool_args.label: Fordham Road Transit Improvements, the Bronx

payload.project_name: Fordham Road, Major Deegan Expressway to Boston Road
payload.description: Investigating transit improvements along Fordham Road between the Major Deegan Expressway and Boston Road. Nine bus routes carry 93,700 daily riders, with connections to subway lines and Metro North. Will evaluate potential design improvements to improve bus speeds and reliability, curb management, and traffic safety.

evidence_refs.source_quote: NYC DOT is investigating transit improvements along Fordham Road between the Major Deegan Expressway and Boston Road.

tool_args.label: Q70 SBS LaGuardia Airport

payload.description: Q70 Select Bus Service from LaGuardia Airport to the Jackson Heights – Roosevelt Avenue subway station and bus terminal.

evidence_refs.source_quote: speed up bus service for approximately 9,000 daily riders on the Q70 SBS

payload.description: Williamsburg Bridge/Washington Plaza-bound bus route, serving Marcy Avenue corridor in Brooklyn.

tool_args.label: Fordham Road Bx12 launched first SBS route in 2008

payload.claim_text: In 2008, NYC DOT and MTA launched the first Select Bus Service (SBS) route on the Bx12, which included curbside bus lanes and off board fare payment. Between 2008 and 2014, Bx12 bus speeds and ridership increased.
payload.description: Historical context of Bx12 Select Bus Service on Fordham Road corridor

evidence_refs.source_quote: In 2008, NYC DOT and MTA launched the first Select Bus Service (SBS) route on the Bx12, which included curbside bus lanes and off board fare payment.

tool_args.label: 79th Street safety and transit benefits

payload.claim_text: Safety benefits include increased pedestrian visibility and accessibility, shorter and safer pedestrian crossings, and slower and safer vehicle turns. Transit benefits include faster boarding and alighting times, improved bus reliability, and new bus stop amenities.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The safety benefits of the project will include increased pedestrian visibility and accessibility, shorter and safer pedestrian crossings, and slower and safer vehicle turns.
